data.content:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the `.cs` file but never adds it to the `Compile` item group, so Roslyn doesn't know about it.

**Fix:** Add a `<Compile>` item for the generated file inside the target:

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but doesn't add it to the `Compile` item group. The file is generated but not included in compilation.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` items using `<ItemGroup>` with a `<Compile Include="..." />` within the target, after the file is written.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->

    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds now. 

**Diagnosis:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote `GeneratedInfo.cs` to the intermediate output directory but never added it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild generated the file, but Roslyn had no idea it existed.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` inside an `<ItemGroup>` in the target so the generated file is included in compilation.
